en: TO THE CHOIRMASTER. A MASKIL OF THE SONS OF KORAH. As a deer pants for flowing streams, so pants my soul for you, O God.
zh: 神啊！我的心渴慕你， 好像鹿渴慕溪水。

en: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I come and appear before God?
zh: 我的心渴想 神，就是永活的 神； 我甚麼時候可以來朝見 神的面呢？

en: My tears have been my food day and night, while they say to me continually, "Where is your God?"
zh: 人整天對我說：“你的 神在哪裡呢？” 我就晝夜以眼淚當飯吃。

en: These things I remember, as I pour out my soul: how I would go with the throng and lead them in procession to the house of God with glad shouts and songs of praise, a multitude keeping festival.
zh: 我從前常常和群眾同去， 與他們進到 神的殿裡， 在歡呼和稱謝聲中，大家守節。 每逢想起這些事，我的心就感到難過。

en: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you in turmoil within me? Hope in God; for I shall again praise him, my salvation
zh: 我的心哪！你為甚麼沮喪呢？ 為甚麼在我裡面不安呢？ 應當等候 神； 因為我還要稱讚他， 他是我面前的救助、我的 神。

en: and my God. My soul is cast down within me; therefore I remember you from the land of Jordan and of Hermon, from Mount Mizar.
zh: 我的心在我裡面沮喪； 因此我從約旦地，從黑門嶺， 從米薩山，記念你。

en: Deep calls to deep at the roar of your waterfalls; all your breakers and your waves have gone over me.
zh: 你的瀑布一發聲，深淵就和深淵響應； 你的洪濤和波浪都掩蓋了我。

en: By day the LORD commands his steadfast love, and at night his song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
zh: 白天耶和華賜下他的慈愛； 夜間我要向他歌頌， 向賜我生命的 神禱告。

en: I say to God, my rock: "Why have you forgotten me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?"
zh: 我要對 神我的磐石說： “你為甚麼忘記我呢？ 我為甚麼因仇敵的壓迫徘徊悲哀呢？”

en: As with a deadly wound in my bones, my adversaries taunt me, while they say to me continually, "Where is your God?"
zh: 我的敵人整天對我說： “你的 神在哪裡呢？” 他們這樣辱罵我的時候， 就像在擊碎我的骨頭。

en: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you in turmoil within me? Hope in God; for I shall again praise him, my salvation and my God.
zh: 我的心哪！你為甚麼沮喪呢？ 為甚麼在我裡面不安呢？ 應當等候 神； 因為我還要稱讚他， 他是我面前的救助、我的 神。
